title: Cells, Walls, Passages, Boundaries, and Invariants · Behavior Case: Boundary Invariant
summary: One cell opens east while its neighbor stays closed west, boundary walls disappear, and rendering data becomes the maze source of truth. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the boundary invariant increment in reciprocal wall and passage model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.
Carve a passage as one atomic reciprocal update between adjacent cells.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 6922 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions. A defensible solution applies Boundary Invariant, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reciprocal wall and passage model.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-wall-cell-invariants-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_wall_cell_invariants_5(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-boundary-invariant-1fuibg
# Competency: maze-boundary-invariant.
# Case 2427: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_boundary_invariant_1fuibg(cells: int = 48, reciprocal_openings: int = 47, passage_edges: int = 47, boundary_breaches: int = 0, illegal_diagonals: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1fuibg = "1fuibg-gbiuf1-2427"
    assert evidence_variant_1fuibg
    evidence_axes_1fuibg = (
        "grid1fuibg",
        "topology2427",
        "searchgbiuf1",
        "event1fuibg2427",
        "access24271fuibg",
        "recoverygbiuf12427",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1fuibg)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0:
        return False, passage_edges, "topology-count"
    if reciprocal_openings != passage_edges:
        return False, reciprocal_openings, "reciprocal-wall"
    if boundary_breaches > 0:
        return False, boundary_breaches, "boundary-policy"
    if illegal_diagonals > 0:
        return False, illegal_diagonals, "illegal-neighbor"
    assert reciprocal_openings == passage_edges
    return True, passage_edges, "valid-wall-model"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Boundary Invariant model · Theory Boundary Invariant, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Boundary Invariant precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Boundary Invariant model · Theory Boundary Invariant, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 4014 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate reciprocal passages, boundary policy, and model topology before rendering; the prior good state must survive failure; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve outer-boundary rules while supporting explicit entrance and exit exceptions.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Boundary Invariant model · Theory Boundary Invariant, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A perfect maze must keep every outside edge closed.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-boundary-invariant-1fuibg(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_boundary_invariant_1fuibg\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or passage_edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reciprocal_openings != passage_edges)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?boundary_breaches > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?illegal_diagonals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
